channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The U.S. and Israeli war on Iran is straining relations with some U.S. allies
item.description: President Trump criticized the U.K. over its stance on the war on Iran, as he praised and welcomed Germany's chancellor to the White House.
item.content:encoded: President Trump criticized the U.K. over its stance on the war on Iran, as he praised and welcomed Germany's chancellor to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: A glimpse of Iran, through the eyes of its artists and journalists
item.description: Understanding one of the world's oldest civilizations can't be achieved through a single film or book. But recent works of literature, journalism, music and film by Iranians are a powerful starting point.
item.content:encoded:
The Seed of the Sacred Fig, For The Sun After Long Nights, Cutting Through Rocks, It Was Just an Accident, Martyr!, and Kayhan Kalhor.'/>

Understanding one of the world's oldest civilizations can't be achieved through a single film or book. But recent works of literature, journalism, music and film by Iranians are a powerful starting point.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: U.S. evacuates diplomats from Middle East. And, what to expect from N.C., Texas primaries
item.description: The U.S. has evacuated diplomats in the Middle East and closed several embassies as war in Iran intensifies. And, what to expect from the Senate races in the North Carolina and Texas primary elections.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. has evacuated diplomats in the Middle East and closed several embassies as war in Iran intensifies. And, what to expect from the Senate races in the North Carolina and Texas primary elections.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: President Trump offers new rationale for U.S. attack on Iran
item.description: The U.S. and Israel have offered different reasons for the latest war in Iran, which has widened as Iran and allied armed groups retaliate across the region.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Israel have offered different reasons for the latest war in Iran, which has widened as Iran and allied armed groups retaliate across the region.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Trump promised the MAGA base no new wars. Then he went to war with Iran
item.description: President Trump promised his "Make America Great Again" voters an "America First" foreign policy. With the war in Iran, he's testing MAGA world's willingness to be flexible on one of its core beliefs.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump promised his "Make America Great Again" voters an "America First" foreign policy. With the war in Iran, he's testing MAGA world's willingness to be flexible on one of its core beliefs.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Melania Trump presides at UN Security Council meeting as U.S. attacks Iran
item.description: U.S. first lady Melania Trump presided over a U.N. Security Council meeting on Monday focusing on children in conflict, as the United States has joined Israel in attacking Iran.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. first lady Melania Trump presided over a U.N. Security Council meeting on Monday focusing on children in conflict, as the United States has joined Israel in attacking Iran.

(Image credit: Angelina Katsanis)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. urges Americans across the Middle East to evacuate as war with Iran intensifies
item.description: The United States evacuated diplomats across the Middle East and shut down some embassies as the war with Iran intensified on Tuesday.
item.content:encoded: The United States evacuated diplomats across the Middle East and shut down some embassies as the war with Iran intensified on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Former Department of Defense official talks about Iran's military capabilities
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Dana Stroul, former deputy assistant secretary of defense for the Middle East, what Iran's current military capabilities are and whether it's retaliating with full force.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Dana Stroul, former deputy assistant secretary of defense for the Middle East, what Iran's current military capabilities are and whether it's retaliating with full force.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Qatari government spokesperson shares his country's view on the U.S. attacks on Iran
item.description: U.S. ally Qatar struck down two Iranian planes Monday. Leila Fadel asks Qatari government spokesperson Majed Al-Ansari about his country's view of U.S. attacks in Iran.
item.content:encoded: U.S. ally Qatar struck down two Iranian planes Monday. Leila Fadel asks Qatari government spokesperson Majed Al-Ansari about his country's view of U.S. attacks in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: France to expand nuclear arsenal, French president says
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron says France will increase its nuclear arsenal and allow the temporary deployment of its nuclear-armed aircraft to other European nations.
item.content:encoded: French President Emmanuel Macron says France will increase its nuclear arsenal and allow the temporary deployment of its nuclear-armed aircraft to other European nations.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley
